title: Roche Sticks with Major Germany Investment While Eli Lilly, Boehringer Cut Spending
summary: Frankfurt: The Swiss pharmaceutical and diagnostics вЂ‹giant Roche said вЂ‹it is sticking to its planned вЂ‹investments in Germany, unlike some of its competitors that have scaled back spending in the...
thbAction: Pitch THB's diagnostics CRM to help labs manage doctor outreach, collection-center expansion, and B2B referral relationships while improving conversion and retention.
contentHtml:
Frankfurt: The Swiss pharmaceutical and diagnostics вЂ‹giant Roche said вЂ‹it is sticking to its planned вЂ‹investments in Germany, unlike some of its competitors that have scaled back spending in the country.

The company вЂЊwill continue вЃ its в‚¬600 вЃ million ($692.88 million) investment in a new diagnostic production вЂ‹site in Penzberg, Germany, Roche told Reuters.

This comes after U.S.-based вЂ‹Eli Lilly said it would halve its $2.3 billion investment in Germany and German drugmaker Boehringer вЂ‹Ingelheim scrapped its в‚¬900 million вЃ plans, both citing вЂЊthe government's planned healthcare вЂ‹cost-cutting measures.

Roche said вЂ‹it would also now have вЃ to review its future investments in Germany вЂ‹carefully.

The decision for Roche's investment in вЂ‹Penzberg, its largest single one in Germany, was made several years ago, with construction expected to be completed by 2027.

"The cabinet decision is creating a new вЂЊdegree of uncertainty regarding investments, research, and production decisions in Germany," Daniel Steiners, вЂ‹CEO of вЂ‹Roche Pharma вЃ AG, told Reuters.

He added that the government risked causing significant economic damage with minimal benefit for a вЂ‹sustainable healthcare system, and said the parliamentary process now offered a final chance to keep Germany on a reliable path.

Source : Reuters
